text: Work done by a force \(\vec{F}\) through displacement \(\vec{s}\) is:
explanation: Work \( W = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{s} = Fs\cos\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{F}\) and \(\vec{s}\).
name: Work Done by Force

options: Net work done on a body = change in kinetic energy
name: Work-Energy Theorem

text: The kinetic energy of a body moving with speed \(v\) is:
explanation: Kinetic energy: \( KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \). If speed doubles, KE quadruples.
name: Kinetic Energy Formula

text: The gravitational potential energy of a body at height \(h\) is:
explanation: \( PE = mgh \), measured from a reference level. This is for small heights where \(g\) is approximately constant.
name: Gravitational PE

options: Work done per unit time: \( P = W/t = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \)
explanation: Power \( P = \frac{dW}{dt} = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \). SI unit: Watt (W) = J/s.
name: Definition of Power

text: A spring of spring constant \(k\) compressed by \(x\) stores elastic PE equal to:
explanation: Elastic PE in spring: \( U = \frac{1}{2}kx^2 \). Work done against spring force \( = \frac{1}{2}kx^2 \).
name: Spring Potential Energy

options: Objects stick together and momentum is conserved
Both KE and momentum are conserved
explanation: Perfectly inelastic: maximum KE loss, objects stick together. Momentum IS conserved (always). KE is NOT conserved.
name: Perfectly Inelastic Collision

text: The coefficient of restitution \(e\) for a perfectly elastic collision is:
explanation: \( e = \frac{\text{relative speed of separation}}{\text{relative speed of approach}} \). Perfectly elastic: \(e=1\), Perfectly inelastic: \(e=0\).
name: Coefficient of Restitution

text: A body of mass 2 kg moving at 3 m/s has kinetic energy:
name: KE Calculation

text: The relation between kinetic energy (KE) and momentum (p) is:
name: KE and Momentum

text: Conservative forces have the property that work done:
options: Is independent of path (depends only on initial and final positions)
Is always maximum on shortest path
explanation: Conservative force (gravity, spring, electrostatic): work done is path-independent. Non-conservative (friction): path-dependent.
name: Conservative Forces

text: A machine with power 500 W lifts a 50 kg mass. Time to lift it to 10 m height (\(g=10\,\text{m/s}^2\)):
name: Power Calculation

text: The escape velocity from Earth is \( v_e = \sqrt{2gR} \approx 11.2\,\text{km/s} \). This is derived from:
options: Kinetic energy = potential energy at surface
Setting total mechanical energy to zero: \( \frac{1}{2}mv_e^2 - \frac{GMm}{R} = 0 \)
name: Escape Velocity Derivation

text: In which case is work done against gravity zero?
options: Moving a body horizontally on a frictionless surface
explanation: Gravity is vertical. Horizontal displacement → \( W_{gravity} = mgh = 0 \) (no vertical displacement). Work by gravity = 0.
name: Zero Work by Gravity

text: If momentum of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes:
explanation: \( KE = p^2/2m \). If \( p \to 2p \): \( KE' = (2p)^2/2m = 4p^2/2m = 4\,KE \). KE becomes 4 times.
name: KE when Momentum Doubled
